skillName: Substituting into expressions with one operation
misconceptionSummary: Students concatenate digits and letters, or substitute the value but apply the wrong operation afterwards.

steps.narration: No, that is not correct. Four t does not become forty-three.
The symbol next to the variable means multiplication. Four t means four times t.
Now calculate correctly. Four times three equals twelve.

lines.text: The operation in the expression tells you what to do

steps.narration: Always follow the operation already written in the expression. Sometimes it is multiplication, sometimes it is addition.

visuals.expression: Substitute the value, then follow the operation shown
visuals.label: 4t with t=3 gives 12, not 43

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, rewrite the expression clearly after substitution and then carry out the correct operation.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: turning 4t into 43 when t = 3. Adjacency means multiplication, so 4t becomes 4 × 3.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If there is no plus or minus sign, a number next to a variable means multiplication.
